Green Index Calculations by Subregion: Karlova District, Tartu, Estonia

Jessica Middleton

March 2026

Introduction

This script calculates Green Index values for the historic residential core of Karlova, Tartu, Estonia, loosely following the methodology described in:

Urban Neighborhood Green Index – A measure of green spaces in urban areas doi: 10.1016/j.landurbplan.2012.01.003

Green Index Definition: The percentage of greenery within each 100m × 100m grid cell, derived from binary classification of NDVI measurements. Cells are classified into four quality levels on a scale of 0.25 - 1:

Green Index Value % Greenness in Cell Label
0 < 25% No Greenness
0.25 25% – 49% Low Greenness
0.50 50% – 74% Medium Greenness
1.00 ≥ 75% High Greenness

Note: The four equal interval classification was adopted for simplicity, as this index provides relative values for comparative evaluation between neighbourhoods rather than absolute values.

Workflow Summary

Software: RStudio (v2025.09.0-387), R (v4.5.1), QGIS (v3.28)
Validation: Results verified post-analysis in QGIS (v3.28)

Data sources:

  • NDVI data (TIFF, 10m × 10m resolution): provided by course instructors via Moodle
  • Study area polygon (GeoPackage): generated by project groupmate

Workflow steps:

  1. Load and reproject NDVI raster and study area polygon to EPSG:3857
  2. Clip NDVI to study area extent
  3. Create 100m × 100m aggregation grid
  4. Binary-classify NDVI (green = NDVI > 0.25) and extract mean greenness per cell
  5. Assign greenness labels from percentage thresholds
  6. Export final masked grid as GeoPackage
  7. Statistical testing (Kruskal-Wallis + Dunn’s post-hoc) by subregion

NOTE: Subregion boundaries were manually delineated in QGIS based on land use polygons. These subregions were joined to each Green Index cell based on location, and the results were saved as a geopackage and imported back into RStudio to continue with statistical testing.

Libraries

library(terra)          # Reading, processing, and analyzing raster and vector spatial data
library(sf)             # Handling vector spatial data
library(exactextractr)  # Extracting raster values within polygons (e.g. NDVI per grid cell)
library(dplyr)          # Data wrangling and manipulation (filtering, grouping, summarizing)
library(ggplot2)        # Creating plots and visualizations (e.g. boxplots)
library(dunn.test)      # Post-hoc Dunn's test following Kruskal-Wallis significance testing
library(multcompView)   # Generating compact letter displays from pairwise comparison results
library(writexl)        # Exporting data frames to Excel (.xlsx) files
library(knitr)          # Table formatting in R Markdown output
library(kableExtra)     # Table formatting in R Markdown output

Data Preparation

Load and Format NDVI Raster

# Fix occasional CRS resolution errors with terra library
Sys.setenv(PROJ_LIB = system.file("proj", package = "terra"))
Sys.setenv(PROJ_DATA = system.file("proj", package = "terra"))

# Load NDVI raster and assign CRS
ndvi <- rast("Tartu_NDVI_2024_ver2.tif")
crs(ndvi) <- "epsg:3857"

# Confirm CRS assigned correctly
crs(ndvi, describe = TRUE)
##                       name authority code                              area
## 1 WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator      EPSG 3857 World between 85.06°S and 85.06°N
##                           extent
## 1 -180.00, 180.00, -85.06, 85.06
# Visualize to confirm alignment with expected data
plot(ndvi, main = "NDVI: Tartu (2024)")

Load and Format Study Area Polygon

# Load study area polygon and reproject to match NDVI CRS
studyarea <- vect("research_area.gpkg")
studyarea <- project(studyarea, crs(ndvi))

# Confirm CRS alignment
crs(studyarea, describe = TRUE)
##                       name authority code                              area
## 1 WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator      EPSG 3857 World between 85.06°S and 85.06°N
##                           extent
## 1 -180.00, 180.00, -85.06, 85.06
print(same.crs(ndvi, studyarea))
## [1] TRUE
# Visualize study area boundary
plot(studyarea, main = "Study Area: Karlova District")

Confirm Initial Alignment

# Overlay NDVI and study area to confirm spatial alignment before clipping
plot(ndvi, main = "NDVI with Study Area Overlay")
plot(studyarea, add = TRUE, col = "red")

Clip NDVI to Study Area

# Mask NDVI to study area extent
ndvi_masked <- mask(ndvi, studyarea)
ndvi_masked <- project(ndvi_masked, crs(ndvi))

# Confirm CRS consistency across all layers
crs(ndvi_masked, describe = TRUE)
##                       name authority code                              area
## 1 WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator      EPSG 3857 World between 85.06°S and 85.06°N
##                           extent
## 1 -180.00, 180.00, -85.06, 85.06
print(same.crs(ndvi, studyarea) & same.crs(ndvi, ndvi_masked))
## [1] TRUE
# Plot masked NDVI with slight margin
bbox         <- st_bbox(studyarea)
x_range      <- bbox["xmax"] - bbox["xmin"]
y_range      <- bbox["ymax"] - bbox["ymin"]
margin_factor <- 0.1

expanded_bbox <- bbox
expanded_bbox["xmin"] <- bbox["xmin"] - margin_factor * x_range
expanded_bbox["xmax"] <- bbox["xmax"] + margin_factor * x_range
expanded_bbox["ymin"] <- bbox["ymin"] - margin_factor * y_range
expanded_bbox["ymax"] <- bbox["ymax"] + margin_factor * y_range

plot(ndvi_masked,
     main = "Masked NDVI: Karlova District",
     xlim = c(expanded_bbox["xmin"], expanded_bbox["xmax"]),
     ylim = c(expanded_bbox["ymin"], expanded_bbox["ymax"]))

Calculate Green Index

Green Index = Percentage of greenness within each 100m × 100m grid cell

% Green Label
< 25% No Greenness
25% – 49% Low Greenness
50% – 74% Medium Greenness
≥ 75% High Greenness

Create 100m × 100m Aggregation Grid

# Report original raster dimensions and resolution
cat("Original raster size:", ncol(ndvi_masked), "columns x", nrow(ndvi_masked), "rows\n")
cat("Original resolution: ", xres(ndvi_masked), "x", yres(ndvi_masked), "map units per pixel\n\n")

# Aggregate to 100m x 100m (10x the original 10m resolution)
new_cell_size_x <- xres(ndvi_masked) * 10
new_cell_size_y <- yres(ndvi_masked) * 10

grid    <- st_make_grid(st_as_sfc(st_bbox(ndvi_masked)),
                        cellsize = c(new_cell_size_x, new_cell_size_y),
                        square = TRUE)
grid_sf <- st_sf(id = seq_along(grid), geometry = grid)

cat("New grid size:", nrow(grid_sf), "cells\n")
cat("New cell size:", new_cell_size_x, "x", new_cell_size_y, "map units\n\n")

# Confirm CRS consistency
print(crs(grid_sf) == crs(ndvi_masked))
## Original raster size: 2055 columns x 1544 rows
## Original resolution:  10 x 10 map units per pixel
## 
## New grid size: 31930 cells
## New cell size: 100 x 100 map units
## 
## [1] TRUE

Extract NDVI Values to Grid

Note: Pixels outside the study area polygon were assigned NaN during masking. Cells with no valid NDVI data are excluded from analysis. Accuracy of the masked dataset was confirmed in QGIS during post-processing

# Binary classification: pixels with NDVI > 0.25 are classified as green
green_pixels <- ndvi_masked > 0.25

# Extract mean greenness per grid cell and convert to percentage
green_percentage       <- exact_extract(green_pixels, grid_sf, 'mean') * 100
##   |                                                                              |                                                                      |   0%  | grid_sf$percent_green  <- round(green_percentage, 2)

# Remove cells with no valid data
valid_grid <- grid_sf[!is.na(grid_sf$percent_green), ]

print(head(valid_grid))
## Simple feature collection with 6 features and 2 fields
## Geometry type: POLYGON
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: 2975620 ymin: 8043600 xmax: 2976220 ymax: 8043700
## Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator
##          id                       geometry percent_green
## 14112 14112 POLYGON ((2975620 8043600, ...         82.86
## 14113 14113 POLYGON ((2975720 8043600, ...         74.58
## 14114 14114 POLYGON ((2975820 8043600, ...         54.41
## 14115 14115 POLYGON ((2975920 8043600, ...         75.00
## 14116 14116 POLYGON ((2976020 8043600, ...         47.06
## 14117 14117 POLYGON ((2976120 8043600, ...         71.28

Assign Greenness Labels

# Classify grid cells into greenness categories based on percentage thresholds
grid_sf$greenness <- cut(
  grid_sf$percent_green,
  breaks = c(0, 25, 50, 75, 100),
  labels = c("No Greenness",
             "Low Greenness",
             "Medium Greenness",
             "High Greenness"),
  include.lowest = TRUE,
  right = FALSE   # intervals are [0,25), [25,50), [50,75), [75,100]
)

Plot Final Green Index and Verify Accuracy

# Define color scheme for greenness labels
colors <- c(
  "No Greenness" = "brown",
  "Low Greenness" = "darkorange",
  "Medium Greenness" = "gold",
  "High Greenness" = "green"
)

# Compute expanded bounding box for plot context
bbox          <- st_bbox(studyarea)
x_range       <- bbox["xmax"] - bbox["xmin"]
y_range       <- bbox["ymax"] - bbox["ymin"]
margin_factor <- 0.1

expanded_bbox          <- bbox
expanded_bbox["xmin"]  <- bbox["xmin"] - margin_factor * x_range
expanded_bbox["xmax"]  <- bbox["xmax"] + margin_factor * x_range
expanded_bbox["ymin"]  <- bbox["ymin"] - margin_factor * y_range
expanded_bbox["ymax"]  <- bbox["ymax"] + margin_factor * y_range

# Remove NA cells, convert to SpatVector, and mask to study area
non_na_grid_sf   <- grid_sf[!is.na(grid_sf$greenness), ]
non_na_grid_vect <- vect(non_na_grid_sf)
masked_grid_sf   <- mask(non_na_grid_vect, studyarea)

# Plot with greenness color scheme
plot(masked_grid_sf["greenness"],
     col      = colors[as.character(masked_grid_sf$greenness)],
     main     = "Green Index: Karlova District, Tartu",
     cex.main = 1.2,
     xlim = c(expanded_bbox["xmin"], expanded_bbox["xmax"]),
     ylim = c(expanded_bbox["ymin"], expanded_bbox["ymax"]))

Export Final SpatVector as GeoPackage

# Uncomment the line below to export the final masked green index layer
# writeVector(masked_grid_sf, "green_index_masked.gpkg", overwrite = TRUE)

The aggregated results from green_index_masked.gpkg were imported into QGIS for further spatial processing. Geographic subregions were then manually delineated with reference to land cover vector data. Each grid cell was assigned to a subregion based on polygon containment. A residual category, Subregion 8, was created for grid cells that did not fall within any of the predefined land cover polygons. The updated GeoPackage was then reimported into R for statistical analysis.

Statistical Testing

The following section tests whether greenness levels differ significantly across subregions of the study area.

Assign Ordinal Green Rank

# Assign ordinal rank (1 = highest greenness, 4 = no greenness) for statistical testing
masked_grid_sf$green_rank <- NA
masked_grid_sf$green_rank[masked_grid_sf$greenness == "High Greenness"]   <- 1
masked_grid_sf$green_rank[masked_grid_sf$greenness == "Medium Greenness"] <- 2
masked_grid_sf$green_rank[masked_grid_sf$greenness == "Low Greenness"]    <- 3
masked_grid_sf$green_rank[masked_grid_sf$greenness == "No Greenness"]     <- 4

head(masked_grid_sf)
##      id percent_green        greenness green_rank
## 1 14112         82.86   High Greenness          1
## 2 14113         74.58 Medium Greenness          2
## 3 14114         54.41 Medium Greenness          2
## 4 14115         75.00   High Greenness          1
## 5 14116         47.06    Low Greenness          3
## 6 14117         71.28 Medium Greenness          2

Load Green Index Output for Statistical Analysis

# Load the exported GeoPackage output for statistical analysis
# This file is generated by the export step above and includes subregion attribution
file <- st_read("green_index_output.gpkg")
## Reading layer `green_index_output' from data source 
##   `C:\Users\jesmi\OneDrive\Desktop\W26_Planning_Project\green_index_output.gpkg' 
##   using driver `GPKG'
## Simple feature collection with 205 features and 4 fields
## Geometry type: POLYGON
## Dimension:     XY
## Bounding box:  xmin: 2975420 ymin: 8043600 xmax: 2976920 ymax: 8045400
## Projected CRS: WGS 84 / Pseudo-Mercator
plot(file, main = "Green Index Output: Subregions")

# Check for missing values before proceeding
cat("Missing log_percgreen values:", sum(is.na(file$log_percgreen)), "\n")
## Missing log_percgreen values: 0
cat("Missing subregion values:",     sum(is.na(file$subregion)), "\n")
## Missing subregion values: 0

Log-Transform Green Percentage

# Log-transform percent_green to reduce right skew before normality testing
# 0.01 is added to handle zero values
file$log_percgreen <- log(file$percent_green + 0.01)

Test for Normality

# Shapiro-Wilk normality test on log-transformed values
# If p < 0.05, data is not normally distributed and non-parametric tests are required
shapiro.test(file$log_percgreen)
## 
##  Shapiro-Wilk normality test
## 
## data:  file$log_percgreen
## W = 0.5395, p-value < 2.2e-16

Kruskal-Wallis Test

# Non-parametric equivalent of one-way ANOVA
# Tests whether greenness distributions differ significantly across subregions
krus_test <- kruskal.test(log_percgreen ~ subregion, data = file)
print(krus_test)
## 
##  Kruskal-Wallis rank sum test
## 
## data:  log_percgreen by subregion
## Kruskal-Wallis chi-squared = 52.664, df = 7, p-value = 4.318e-09

Post-Hoc Dunn’s Test

# Dunn's test with Bonferroni correction identifies which subregions differ significantly
dunn_res <- dunn.test(file$log_percgreen, file$subregion, method = "bonferroni")

Compact Letter Display and Boxplot

# Order subregion factor levels alphabetically 
file$subregion <- factor(file$subregion, levels = sort(unique(file$subregion)))

# Extract adjusted p-values and format comparison names for multcompLetters
pvals        <- dunn_res$P.adjusted
comparisons  <- dunn_res$comparisons
names(pvals) <- gsub(" vs ", "-", comparisons)

# Generate compact letter display 
cld <- multcompLetters(pvals, threshold = 0.05)

# Build summary dataframe by subregion
summary_df <- file %>%
  group_by(subregion) %>%
  summarize(median = median(log_percgreen, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  mutate(letter = cld$Letters[as.character(subregion)])

# Plot boxplot with Dunn's test letters annotated
ggplot(file, aes(x = factor(subregion), y = log_percgreen)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_text(data = summary_df,
            aes(x = factor(subregion), y = median + 0.1, label = letter),
            colour = "red", size = 5) +
  labs(x     = "Subregion",
       y     = "Log-transformed % Green Cover",
       title = "Green Cover by Subregion: Dunn's Test Post-Hoc Letters") +
  expand_limits(y = max(summary_df$median + 1)) +
  theme_minimal()

Greenness Proportion Table by Subregion

# Define the all_levels vector (in case not already in environment)
all_levels <- c("No Greenness", "Low Greenness", "Medium Greenness", "High Greenness")

# Create proportion_table 
proportion_table <- file %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(green_connectivity)) %>%
  mutate(green_connectivity = factor(green_connectivity, levels = all_levels)) %>%
  count(subregion, green_connectivity, .drop = FALSE) %>%
  group_by(subregion) %>%
  mutate(percentage = round((n / sum(n)) * 100, 2)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Define colors for the 8 subregions
subregion_colors <- c(
  "Subregion 1" = "#D1E9F6", "Subregion 2" = "#F6EACB", 
  "Subregion 3" = "#D1D8C5", "Subregion 4" = "#EADBC8", 
  "Subregion 5" = "#F1D4E5", "Subregion 6" = "#E1AFD1", 
  "Subregion 7" = "#FFE6E6", "Subregion 8" = "#E5E1DA"
)

# Generate and style table
proportion_table %>%
  mutate(
    subregion = cell_spec(subregion, "html", 
                          background = subregion_colors[subregion],
                          bold = TRUE,
                          color = "#333333")
  ) %>%
  kable(
    format = "html", 
    escape = FALSE, 
    caption = "Greenness Classification by Subregion",
    col.names = c("Subregion", "Greenness Classification", "Count", "Percentage (%)"),
    align = "llrr"
  ) %>%
  kable_styling(
    bootstrap_options = c("hover", "condensed"),
    full_width = TRUE
  ) %>%
  column_spec(1, width = "20%", extra_css = "padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;") %>%
  column_spec(2, width = "30%", extra_css = "padding-left: 20px; padding-right: 20px;") %>%
  column_spec(3:4, width = "25%", extra_css = "padding-right: 30px;") %>%
  collapse_rows(columns = 1, valign = "middle")
